description: This figure presents an energy dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) elemental mapping result for a small oxide nodule generated on Fe-15Mn-13Al alloy (run 11) after isothermal oxidation under 200 torr pure oxygen at the tested high temperature. The bright regions represent areas of high concentration of the mapped element, showing significant manganese enrichment within the oxide nodule, while an aluminum-rich oxide layer is confirmed to exist at the interface between the oxide nodule and the alloy substrate. This observation directly verifies that for Fe-Mn-Al alloys with aluminum content higher than 9 wt%, manganese-containing oxide nodules can penetrate the initially formed protective alumina scale during oxidation, supporting the proposed oxidation behavior mechanism for high-aluminum Fe-Mn-Al alloys in this work.
